review_notes: The functions generally map well to the control RA-5 (5) focusing on vulnerability monitoring and privileged access. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but a minor change was made to improve clarity and consistency. The functions accurately reflect Kubernetes security practices related to vulnerability scanning and access control.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'restricted' instead of 'deny' for clarity in k8s_admission_controller_privileged_access_deny
title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning | Privileged Access
step1_reasoning: The control RA-5 (5) focuses on vulnerability monitoring and scanning with an emphasis on privileged access. In Kubernetes, this maps to ensuring that images are scanned for vulnerabilities before deployment (k8s_image_vulnerability_scanning_enabled), controlling privileged access through admission controllers (k8s_admission_controller_privileged_access_deny), and enforcing least privilege in RBAC configurations (k8s_rbac_least_privilege_enforcement). These checks collectively address the requirement for monitoring vulnerabilities and managing privileged access.
